model_summary: Criterion set C01–C06 captures daytime meal omission, nocturnal hunger, impulsive late-night food choices, interoceptive hunger-signal reliability, and absence/presence of dieting goals. Predictor set P01–P04 targets meal planning/structure, mindful eating, eating-environment design, and planned snack structure. This compact, non-redundant configuration yields measurable within-person variance via momentary EMA (1–9 Likert scales where appropriate) and supports gVAR estimation with clearly specified sampling, timing, and lag structure.
variable_selection_notes: Variables chosen to cover (i) daytime meal initiation and structure (C01, C06, C04), (ii) nocturnal eating drive and choices (C02, C03), and (iii) dieting/personal rules context (C05). Predictors focus on actionable levers with established links to the complaint domain in HyDe and LLM-mapped paths: meal planning/structure, mindful eating, environment design, and planned snack structure. Trade-offs include balancing ecological validity with model parsimony; all included items are modifiable through behavioral interventions and yield sufficient within-person variance.

momentary_ema.notes: Six prompts/day over 14 days to capture daytime feeding patterns, hunger spikes, and eating choices with jitter within blocks to reduce anticipation bias.

daily_diary.notes: Evening diary capturing hunger cues, perceived hunger-signal reliability, and reflections on dieting thoughts.

passive_streams.notes: Passive streams disabled to retain feasibility and minimize missing data risk in initial model.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Initial variable set described below is compact and estimable; edges implemented to enable a coherent gVAR starting point.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: 1–9 Likert scales (where used) provide within-person variance across the day; planned meal structure and environment design are expected to fluctuate with context (workdays vs weekends).
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: EMA fatigue is a consideration; reminders and reasonable daily quotas are recommended to minimize missingness.

safety_notes: Not a diagnosis. No medical dosing or interventions; monitor for acute risk and escalate to care if self-harm or safety concerns arise.

criteria_variables.label: Forgetting to eat during the day

measurement.item_or_signal: During the current daytime period, to what extent did you forget to eat or skip meals today? (1=not at all to 9=extremely often)
measurement.notes: Atomic item targeting daytime meal initiation; aligns with C01 evidence and decompensation.

criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: The variable C01 item 'forgetting to eat during the day' is a daytime meal-initiation symptom captured by a momentary EMA; none of the candidate leaves exactly represent daytime meal initiation or daytime forgetting to eat. Most leaves pertain to night eating, sleep-related eating disorders, or unrelated eating symptoms; the closest is 'daytime_memory_impairment' (26318) but that is a generalized cognitive impairment, not a meal-initiation criterion. Therefore no clear leaf matches; unmapped.
criteria_variables.unmapped_reason: No candidate leaf directly corresponds to daytime meal initiation failure/forgetting to eat during the day. The closest is daytime_memory_impairment (26318), which is not meal-specific, so mapping would be inaccurate.
criteria_variables.mapping_query_text_used: Forgetting to eat during the day | Operational item/signal: During the current daytime period, to what extent did you forget to eat or skip meals today? (1=not at all to 9=extremely often) | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Atomic item targeting daytime meal initiation; aligns with C01 evidence and decompensation.

criteria_variables.label: Large hunger increase at night (compensatory starvation)

measurement.item_or_signal: In the evening/night, how strong is your hunger compared with the daytime? (1=not hungry to 9=extremely hungry)
measurement.notes: Captures nocturnal hunger surges following daytime under-eating.

criteria_variables.label: Making poor food choices when very hungry

measurement.item_or_signal: When very hungry at night, how often do you choose energy-dense or less nutritious foods? (1=never to 9=very often)
measurement.notes: Direct link to nocturnal impulsive eating behavior.

criteria_variables.mapped_rationale: Pseudoprofile maps C03 to nocturnal eating drive/choices. The variable assesses nighttime hungry moments and frequency of choosing energy-dense or less nutritious foods. Among candidates, 26803 corresponds to night_time eating variant (binge-focused) with nighttime_craving_for_high_calorie_foods, which is the most specific operational construct matching nocturnal food-choice behavior (energy-dense foods) during hunger. Measurement mode EMA_self_report aligns (momentary, 1–9 scale), smartphone EMA, 6 prompts/day, recalling since last prompt. This best captures the nocturnal eating impulsivity/choice mechanism within C03.

criteria_variables.label: Perceived breakdown of internal hunger cues

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, to what extent do you feel your hunger cues are reliable or accurate? (1=very reliable to 9=not reliable at all)
measurement.notes: Assesses interoceptive signal quality as a target for interoception training.

measurement.notes: Diet-identity/intentional restriction is context-dependent; polarity treated as bidirectional in initial model.

measurement.notes: Targets daytime planning/organization that contribute to missed meals.

predictor_variables.label: Meal_Planning_Structure
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets daytime meal initiation and scheduling; improves caloric intake regularity and satiety signaling.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Meal-reminders, calendar-based meal templates, auto-snack prompts

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, did you plan to eat at scheduled times (meals/snacks)?
measurement.notes: Captures daytime planning fidelity.

expected_effects.rationale: Increased daytime planning reduces daytime meal omission within hours.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Best-matching leaf for Meal_Planning_Structure: directly reflects daytime meal planning and snack scheduling. The leaf 6222 BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / planned_snack_structure captures planned snack structure within the eating-behavior domain, aligning with the variable label. Measurement item 'Today, did you plan to eat at scheduled times (meals/snacks)?' is an EMA self-report capturing planning fidelity for meals and snacks, matching the variable's mechanism to improve caloric intake regularity and satiety signaling. Among candidates, this is the most specific operational construct for meal planning/structure relevant to the predictor set.

predictor_variables.label: Mindful_Eating_Practice
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Enhances interoceptive awareness and restraint cues; reduces impulsive choices during hunger.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief mindful-eating prompts; DBT mindfulness skills training; informal mindful-eating practice

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, did you practice mindful eating (checking hunger/fullness cues before meals)?
measurement.notes: Represents a cognitive-skill based predictor.

expected_effects.rationale: Mindful eating reduces daytime meal omission via cue-awareness.

feasibility.notes: Feasible with brief prompts; training may be needed.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: Mindful_Eating_Practice directly maps to the leaf mindful_eating_practice under PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_based_interventions / informal_everyday_mindfulness, which corresponds to the measurement item 'Today, did you practice mindful eating (checking hunger/fullness cues before meals)?' and uses an EMA 1–9 scale. This is the most specific operational construct among candidates for a direct mindful-eating predictor (P02). Mechanism aligns with enhanced interoceptive awareness and restraint cues to reduce impulsive choices during hunger; measurement context matches EMA self-report as described in the pseudoprofile.

predictor_variables.label: Environment_Design_For_Eating
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Modifies eating context to reduce temptations and improve healthy choice likelihood.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Arrange counter-top snack options, remove tempting items from sight, create dedicated eating zones

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, was your eating environment designed to support healthy choices (e.g., no tempting foods within easy reach)?
measurement.notes: Addresses environmental constraints on eating behavior.

expected_effects.rationale: Improved environment design lowers nocturnal hunger-driven impulsivity.

feasibility.notes: Implementation is simple but may require household-level adjustments.

predictor_variables.mapped_rationale: The variable Environment_Design_For_Eating directly corresponds to BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / environment_design_for_eating. The measurement item asks whether the eating environment supports healthy choices and uses EMA self-report on a 1-9 scale, with six prompts per day, consistent with the leaf's operational design. The mechanism aligns with how environment design can alter temptations and healthy choice likelihood, matching the environment_design_for_eating leaf. This is the most specific match among candidates.

predictor_variables.label: Planned_Snack_Structure
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Structured snacking can smooth hunger fluctuations and reduce night- time overcompensation.

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you plan a snack today at a specific time? (1=not planned to 9=fully planned)
measurement.notes: Directly supports hunger-signal stabilization.

expected_effects.rationale: Structured snacks reduce nocturnal overeating by providing predictable energy intake.

feasibility.notes: Feasible with simple prompts and templates.

source_variable.label: Forgetting to eat during the day

measurement.item_or_signal: During the current daytime period, to what extent did you forget to eat or skip meals today? (1=not at all to 9=extremely often)
measurement.notes: Atomic item targeting daytime meal initiation; aligns with C01 evidence and decompensation.

criteria.label: Large hunger increase at night (compensatory starvation)
criteria.query_text_used: Large hunger increase at night (compensatory starvation) | Operational item/signal: In the evening/night, how strong is your hunger compared with the daytime? (1=not hungry to 9=extremely hungry) | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Captures nocturnal hunger surges following daytime under-eating.

mapping.rationale: Variable label indicates nocturnal hunger surge. The measurement item asks about hunger strength at night, aligning with nighttime craving. Among candidates, 26634 corresponds to nighttime_food_craving within night eating syndrome (osfed), providing the most specific operational match for nocturnal hunger/craving measured via EMA. This leaf best captures the intended mechanism (nocturnal hunger driving night eating behaviors) without overfitting to a broader or non-night-specific construct.

source_variable.label: Large hunger increase at night (compensatory starvation)

measurement.item_or_signal: In the evening/night, how strong is your hunger compared with the daytime? (1=not hungry to 9=extremely hungry)
measurement.notes: Captures nocturnal hunger surges following daytime under-eating.

criteria.label: Making poor food choices when very hungry
criteria.query_text_used: Making poor food choices when very hungry | Operational item/signal: When very hungry at night, how often do you choose energy-dense or less nutritious foods? (1=never to 9=very often) | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Direct link to nocturnal impulsive eating behavior.

mapping.rationale: Pseudoprofile maps C03 to nocturnal eating drive/choices. The variable assesses nighttime hungry moments and frequency of choosing energy-dense or less nutritious foods. Among candidates, 26803 corresponds to night_time eating variant (binge-focused) with nighttime_craving_for_high_calorie_foods, which is the most specific operational construct matching nocturnal food-choice behavior (energy-dense foods) during hunger. Measurement mode EMA_self_report aligns (momentary, 1–9 scale), smartphone EMA, 6 prompts/day, recalling since last prompt. This best captures the nocturnal eating impulsivity/choice mechanism within C03.

source_variable.label: Making poor food choices when very hungry

measurement.item_or_signal: When very hungry at night, how often do you choose energy-dense or less nutritious foods? (1=never to 9=very often)
measurement.notes: Direct link to nocturnal impulsive eating behavior.

criteria.label: Perceived breakdown of internal hunger cues
criteria.query_text_used: Perceived breakdown of internal hunger cues | Operational item/signal: Today, to what extent do you feel your hunger cues are reliable or accurate? (1=very reliable to 9=not reliable at all) | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Assesses interoceptive signal quality as a target for interoception training.

mapping.rationale: Variable measures perceived reliability of internal hunger cues (C04) via EMA; best leaf mapping is interoceptive_hypervigilance_gastric_signals (hunger-related interoceptive cues) which captures attention/monitoring of gastric hunger signals and interoceptive signal reliability. The item 'Today, to what extent do you feel your hunger cues are reliable or accurate?' aligns with hypervigilance to gastric cues; measurement is momentary self-report via smartphone EMA, consistent with the leaf's mechanism; this is the most specific operational construct among candidates for hunger cue reliability within the criterion set.

source_variable.label: Perceived breakdown of internal hunger cues

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, to what extent do you feel your hunger cues are reliable or accurate? (1=very reliable to 9=not reliable at all)
measurement.notes: Assesses interoceptive signal quality as a target for interoception training.

criteria.label: Lack of intentional food restriction or dieting
criteria.query_text_used: Lack of intentional food restriction or dieting | Operational item/signal: Today, did you intentionally restrict calories or follow any diet rules? (1=not at all to 9=absolutely yes) | Polarity: unknown | Notes: Diet-identity/intentional restriction is context-dependent; polarity treated as bidirectional in initial model.

mapping.rationale: The variable describes lack of intentional caloric restriction or dieting. The EMA self-report item directly probes whether the person intentionally restricted calories or followed dieting rules today. The most specific CRITERION leaf that matches this is dietary_autonomy, representing autonomy over dietary choices and absence of imposed dieting rules. This leaf best maps the bidirectional polarity and momentary assessment context (EMA_self_report) to the intended construct of not restricting intake.

source_variable.label: Lack of intentional food restriction or dieting

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, did you intentionally restrict calories or follow any diet rules? (1=not at all to 9=absolutely yes)
measurement.notes: Diet-identity/intentional restriction is context-dependent; polarity treated as bidirectional in initial model.

criteria.label: Daytime attention/organizational factors contributing to missed meals
criteria.query_text_used: Daytime attention/organizational factors contributing to missed meals | Operational item/signal: Today, how well were you able to plan or attend to meal timing (e.g., reminders, structure) during daytime? (1=not at all organized to 9=very organized) | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Targets daytime planning/organization that contribute to missed meals.

mapping.rationale: Variable label refers to daytime planning/structure affecting meal timing. Measurement asks about planning/reminders during daytime, matching the leaf 'creating schedules' under time management. This is the most specific operational construct for daytime meal planning and organization in EMA context.

source_variable.label: Daytime attention/organizational factors contributing to missed meals

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, how well were you able to plan or attend to meal timing (e.g., reminders, structure) during daytime? (1=not at all organized to 9=very organized)
measurement.notes: Targets daytime planning/organization that contribute to missed meals.

predictors.label: Meal_Planning_Structure
predictors.query_text_used: Meal_Planning_Structure | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Directly targets daytime meal initiation and scheduling; improves caloric intake regularity and satiety signaling. | Intervention examples: Meal-reminders, calendar-based meal templates, auto-snack prompts | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today, did you plan to eat at scheduled times (meals/snacks)? | Prior ontology hint: BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / meal_planning_and_structure

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf for Meal_Planning_Structure: directly reflects daytime meal planning and snack scheduling. The leaf 6222 BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / planned_snack_structure captures planned snack structure within the eating-behavior domain, aligning with the variable label. Measurement item 'Today, did you plan to eat at scheduled times (meals/snacks)?' is an EMA self-report capturing planning fidelity for meals and snacks, matching the variable's mechanism to improve caloric intake regularity and satiety signaling. Among candidates, this is the most specific operational construct for meal planning/structure relevant to the predictor set.

source_variable.label: Meal_Planning_Structure
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets daytime meal initiation and scheduling; improves caloric intake regularity and satiety signaling.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Meal-reminders, calendar-based meal templates, auto-snack prompts

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, did you plan to eat at scheduled times (meals/snacks)?
measurement.notes: Captures daytime planning fidelity.

expected_effects.rationale: Increased daytime planning reduces daytime meal omission within hours.

predictors.label: Mindful_Eating_Practice
predictors.query_text_used: Mindful_Eating_Practice | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Enhances interoceptive awareness and restraint cues; reduces impulsive choices during hunger. | Intervention examples: Brief mindful-eating prompts; DBT mindfulness skills training; informal mindful-eating practice | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today, did you practice mindful eating (checking hunger/fullness cues before meals)? | Prior ontology hint: PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_skills / *mindful_eating_practice

mapping.rationale: Mindful_Eating_Practice directly maps to the leaf mindful_eating_practice under PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / mindfulness_based_interventions / informal_everyday_mindfulness, which corresponds to the measurement item 'Today, did you practice mindful eating (checking hunger/fullness cues before meals)?' and uses an EMA 1–9 scale. This is the most specific operational construct among candidates for a direct mindful-eating predictor (P02). Mechanism aligns with enhanced interoceptive awareness and restraint cues to reduce impulsive choices during hunger; measurement context matches EMA self-report as described in the pseudoprofile.

source_variable.label: Mindful_Eating_Practice
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Enhances interoceptive awareness and restraint cues; reduces impulsive choices during hunger.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Brief mindful-eating prompts; DBT mindfulness skills training; informal mindful-eating practice

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, did you practice mindful eating (checking hunger/fullness cues before meals)?
measurement.notes: Represents a cognitive-skill based predictor.

expected_effects.rationale: Mindful eating reduces daytime meal omission via cue-awareness.

feasibility.notes: Feasible with brief prompts; training may be needed.

predictors.label: Environment_Design_For_Eating
predictors.query_text_used: Environment_Design_For_Eating | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Modifies eating context to reduce temptations and improve healthy choice likelihood. | Intervention examples: Arrange counter-top snack options, remove tempting items from sight, create dedicated eating zones | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today, was your eating environment designed to support healthy choices (e.g., no tempting foods within easy reach)? | Prior ontology hint: BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / environment_design_for_eating

mapping.rationale: The variable Environment_Design_For_Eating directly corresponds to BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / environment_design_for_eating. The measurement item asks whether the eating environment supports healthy choices and uses EMA self-report on a 1-9 scale, with six prompts per day, consistent with the leaf's operational design. The mechanism aligns with how environment design can alter temptations and healthy choice likelihood, matching the environment_design_for_eating leaf. This is the most specific match among candidates.

source_variable.label: Environment_Design_For_Eating
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Modifies eating context to reduce temptations and improve healthy choice likelihood.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Arrange counter-top snack options, remove tempting items from sight, create dedicated eating zones

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, was your eating environment designed to support healthy choices (e.g., no tempting foods within easy reach)?
measurement.notes: Addresses environmental constraints on eating behavior.

expected_effects.rationale: Improved environment design lowers nocturnal hunger-driven impulsivity.

feasibility.notes: Implementation is simple but may require household-level adjustments.

predictors.label: Planned_Snack_Structure
predictors.query_text_used: Planned_Snack_Structure | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Structured snacking can smooth hunger fluctuations and reduce night- time overcompensation. | Intervention examples: Pre-planned snack templates, snack-prep routines | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Did you plan a snack today at a specific time? (1=not planned to 9=fully planned) | Prior ontology hint: BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / *planned_snack_structure

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf: BIO / nutrition_hydration_and_metabolic_health / eating_behavior_and_satiety_skills / planned_snack_structure. Variable label Planned_Snack_Structure maps directly to this leaf's planned_snack_structure slot. EMA_self_report measurement (Did you plan a snack today at a specific time?, 1-9) with 6 prompts/day, recall since_last_prompt; Directly supports hunger-signal stabilization. Mechanism: planned snack structure can smooth hunger fluctuations and reduce nocturnal overeating, aligning with the predictor's aim to influence daytime meal initiation/structure and nocturnal eating drive via planning tools.

source_variable.label: Planned_Snack_Structure
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Structured snacking can smooth hunger fluctuations and reduce night- time overcompensation.

measurement.item_or_signal: Did you plan a snack today at a specific time? (1=not planned to 9=fully planned)
measurement.notes: Directly supports hunger-signal stabilization.

expected_effects.rationale: Structured snacks reduce nocturnal overeating by providing predictable energy intake.

feasibility.notes: Feasible with simple prompts and templates.
